Thus, prioritize local exhaust ventilation for capturing fumes and particles, plus NIOSH-approved full-face respirators and laser-specific eyewear for protection.

analysis.question: How does Tungsten's density and thermal conductivity influence the heat-affected zone during laser surface treatment?
analysis.answer: Notably, tungsten's high density (19.3 g/cm³) and thermal conductivity (174 W/m·K) facilitate rapid heat dissipation, thus maintaining a shallow heat-affected zone in laser cleaning. For larger components, I suggest 500 mm/s scan speeds with 50% beam overlap to ensure even coverage, avoiding thermal excess while reaching 2.5 J/cm² fluence for oxide removal.

To counter this, apply multi-pass strategies—three scans at 2.5 J/cm² fluence with 50% overlap for uniform ablation. Thus, verify outcomes via XPS to identify any residual contaminants.
